articles.title: Arizona Files Illegal-Gambling Charges Against Predictions Platform Kalshi
articles.summary: Arizona prosecutors charged prediction market Kalshi with illegal gambling, marking the first criminal case against a major regulated prediction platform. The move signals state authorities are willing to challenge federally-approved betting markets, potentially forcing other platforms to reconsider which events they'll allow users to trade.

articles.title: Polymarket bettors tried to make a reporter change his story about Iran missile strike — or else
articles.summary: Polymarket traders apparently threatened a journalist to pressure him into altering his reporting on Iran's missile strike, using their betting positions as leverage. This shows how high-stakes prediction markets can create perverse incentives where bettors try to manipulate the news events they're supposed to be predicting.

articles.title: Betting on the Oscars or the Super Bowl halftime show would be banned under new prediction market bill
articles.summary: A new bill would prohibit betting on entertainment events like the Oscars and Super Bowl halftime performances while still allowing prediction markets on political and economic outcomes. The legislation signals lawmakers want to draw clear lines between "serious" forecasting markets and what they view as frivolous gambling on pop culture spectacles.

articles.title: Major prediction market faces 34th country ban
articles.summary: Another country has banned a major prediction market platform, bringing the total number of jurisdictions blocking access to 34. The growing regulatory crackdown signals that governments view prediction markets as gambling rather than information aggregation tools, forcing platforms to choose between compliance costs and global reach.

articles.title: U.S. Democratic lawmakers introduce bill to crack down on prediction markets
articles.summary: Democratic lawmakers want to restrict prediction markets, likely targeting platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket that have exploded in popularity during election season. If it gains traction, this could force major platforms to delist political markets or face regulatory crackdowns, potentially pushing the action back to offshore sites where U.S. bettors already wager billions.

articles.title: Betting on 5-minute swings on Bitcoin price are the hot new thing on prediction markets
articles.summary: Ultra-short Bitcoin prediction markets are exploding in popularity, letting traders bet on whether the price will move up or down in just 5-minute windows. This marks a major shift toward gambling-style instant gratification that could either bring massive new volume to prediction platforms or turn them into glorified casinos that regulators will crush.

articles.title: Make the Predictions Come True
articles.summary: Some prediction markets are shifting from pure forecasting to actively trying to influence the outcomes they're betting on. This blurs the line between predicting and manipulating events, which could either supercharge market accuracy or completely undermine their credibility as information sources.

articles.title: Perfection is nearly impossible: Kalshi is giving away $1 billion for a perfect bracket
articles.summary: Kalshi is offering $1 billion to anyone who can correctly predict every game in March Madness, joining the long tradition of impossible bracket challenges that generate massive publicity while costing almost nothing. The stunt shows how prediction markets are using lottery-style promotions to attract mainstream attention, even though the mathematical odds make Warren Buffett's old $1 billion bracket bet look generous.

articles.title: Why Does The CFTC Chair Want People To Bet On Drug Trials At Prediction Markets?
articles.summary: CFTC Chair Rostin Behnam is pushing for prediction markets to cover pharmaceutical drug trials, letting people bet on whether experimental treatments will succeed or fail. This could unlock massive liquidity in healthcare betting while giving researchers and investors better real-time data on which drugs actually have a shot at making it to market.

articles.title: Traders Flip Senate Control Bet as Democrats Overtake Republicans on Kalshi, Polymarket
articles.summary: Prediction market traders are now betting Democrats will control the Senate after the 2024 elections, with the party's odds surging past Republicans on both Kalshi and Polymarket. This flip signals either new polling data, campaign developments, or smart money spotting value that the broader market missed.

articles.title: Polymarket Five-Minute Bets Capture ‘Addictive’ Crypto Craze
articles.summary: Polymarket has launched ultra-short five-minute betting windows that are drawing comparisons to gambling addiction rather than thoughtful prediction markets. The move signals how crypto prediction platforms are chasing dopamine-driven volume over the deliberative forecasting that made them credible alternatives to traditional betting.

articles.title: AI agents are quietly rewriting prediction market trading
articles.summary: Automated AI trading systems are increasingly dominating prediction market activity, executing trades faster and more systematically than human participants. This shift threatens to price out retail bettors while potentially making markets more efficient but less accessible to the average person looking to profit from their insights.
